summary: High‑energy set with stellar To Lay Me Down and tight second‑set jams
blurb: The show delivers a blazing guitar solo on “To Lay Me Down,” a crisp “Bertha,” and a propulsive second set that includes a seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition. While the first set has occasional flat moments, the high‑energy finish makes it a must‑listen.
review:
May 4, 1981 at The Spectrum captured the Dead in top form, especially on the second half of the night. Jerry’s six‑minute “To Lay Me Down” solo crackles like “purple lightning bolt,” and his tone stays on fire through the blazing “Bertha.” The classic “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” segue is tight, though a few reviewers note Bob jumping ahead on vocals. Brent’s harmonies sit solidly under the mix, while Mickey’s drumming—often described as a “pizza‑shaped drum” standing next to Jerry—holds down the groove after an early bass‑thin opening.

The soundboard source (gd81-05-04.sbd.clugston.2250.sbeok.shnf) offers the clearest picture of this high‑energy performance despite some hiss and thin low end, making it the go‑to recording. The matrix capture reveals additional gems—“Althea,” “Black Peter,” and a memorable “Cold Rain And Snow > Little Red Rooster” sequence—while an audience FOB tape showcases the punchy front‑of‑house vibe. Overall, the show is a strong second‑set showcase with enough highlights to satisfy both archivists and casual listeners.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings were analyzed; three are clearly positive and one is mixed, with multiple reviewers praising standout songs (To Lay Me Down, Althea, Black Peter) and the overall energy of the show. The single mixed review notes vocal issues but does not detract from the consensus of a strong performance, justifying a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑sound soundboard capture that highlights the high‑energy performance and key songs like “To Lay Me Down,” while maintaining overall clarity despite minor hiss.

key_highlights: Sublime "To Lay Me Down" solo with blazing guitar tone.
Ripping, fast‑tempo second set featuring "Bertha" and a tight China Cat > I Know You Rider transition.
Matrix source uncovers gems such as "Althea," "Black Peter," and the "Cold Rain And Snow > Little Red Rooster" sequence.
Punchy front‑of‑house audience recording adds extra body and high‑end sparkle.

band_performance.Jerry: Blazing guitar tone dominates the night, highlighted by a six‑minute solo on “To Lay Me Down”; occasional drop‑outs during the China Cat jam but overall stays fire‑hot. Vocals generally stronger than Bob’s.
band_performance.Bob: Hoarse voice seen as an improvement by some listeners yet criticized for jumping ahead in the China Cat jam and singing ahead of Jerry; vocal performance described as horrible except on “Little Red Rooster" where he shines.
band_performance.Brent: Solid harmonies and keyboard work that follow the band without major issues, providing a steady backdrop throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Stands next to Jerry with a "pizza‑shaped drum," delivering solid drumming after an early thin‑bass section; overall percussion remains tight.
